text.title: GitLab Issue Boards Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding GitLab Issue Boards. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Status states MUST be modelled with GitLab scoped labels (`status::`). Free-form labels for status are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Free-form status labels collide; scoped labels are mutually exclusive within their scope, matching kanban semantics.

rule.statement: Agents MUST authenticate via project-scoped tokens with `api` + `read_repository` minimum. Group or instance tokens forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group/instance tokens are over-privileged; project-scoped tokens contain blast radius if leaked.

rule.statement: Milestones MUST follow a fixed cadence (weekly / biweekly / monthly). Ad-hoc milestone creation forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc milestones produce inconsistent velocity metrics; fixed cadence makes comparison possible.

rule.statement: Each board list MUST be backed by a single scoped-label value. Free-form lists forbidden.
rule.rationale: Free-form lists drift; scoped-label-backed lists stay synchronised with status taxonomy.

rule.statement: Each issue MUST be assigned to EITHER an iteration OR a milestone, not both. Dual assignment creates conflicting reports.
rule.rationale: Iteration + milestone double-counting inflates velocity and confuses reporting tools.

rule.statement: If preconditions for GitLab Issue Boards fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying GitLab Issue Boards outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
